root_text.an6.50:1.1: “Indriyasaṁvare, bhikkhave, asati indriyasaṁvaravipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti sīlaṁ;
root_text.an6.50:1.2: sīle asati sīlavipannassa hatūpaniso hoti sammāsamādhi;
root_text.an6.50:1.3: sammāsamādhimhi asati sammāsamādhivipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti yathābhūtañāṇadassanaṁ;
root_text.an6.50:1.4: yathābhūtañāṇadassane asati yathābhūtañāṇadassanavipannassa hatūpaniso hoti nibbidāvirāgo;
root_text.an6.50:1.5: nibbidāvirāge asati nibbidāvirāgavipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti vimuttiñāṇadassanaṁ.
root_text.an6.50:1.7: Tassa papaṭikāpi na pāripūriṁ gacchati, tacopi na pāripūriṁ gacchati, pheggupi na pāripūriṁ gacchati, sāropi na pāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.an6.50:1.8: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, indriyasaṁvare asati indriyasaṁvaravipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti sīlaṁ …pe…
root_text.an6.50:2.1: Indriyasaṁvare, bhikkhave, sati indriyasaṁvarasampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti sīlaṁ;
root_text.an6.50:2.2: sīle sati sīlasampannassa upanisasampanno hoti sammāsamādhi;
root_text.an6.50:2.3: sammāsamādhimhi sati sammāsamādhisampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti yathābhūtañāṇadassanaṁ;
root_text.an6.50:2.4: yathābhūtañāṇadassane sati yathābhūtañāṇadassanasampannassa upanisasampanno hoti nibbidāvirāgo;
root_text.an6.50:2.5: nibbidāvirāge sati nibbidāvirāgasampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti vimuttiñāṇadassanaṁ.
root_text.an6.50:2.6: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, rukkho sākhāpalāsasampanno. Tassa papaṭikāpi pāripūriṁ gacchati, tacopi pāripūriṁ gacchati, pheggupi pāripūriṁ gacchati, sāropi pāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.an6.50:2.7: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, indriyasaṁvare sati indriyasaṁvarasampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti sīlaṁ …pe…

translation_text.an6.50:1.1: “Mendicants, when there is no sense restraint, one who lacks sense restraint has destroyed a vital condition for ethical conduct.
translation_text.an6.50:1.2: When there is no ethical conduct, one who lacks ethics has destroyed a vital condition for right immersion.
translation_text.an6.50:1.3: When there is no right immersion, one who lacks right immersion has destroyed a vital condition for true knowledge and vision.
translation_text.an6.50:1.4: When there is no true knowledge and vision, one who lacks true knowledge and vision has destroyed a vital condition for disillusionment and dispassion.
translation_text.an6.50:1.5: When there is no disillusionment and dispassion, one who lacks disillusionment and dispassion has destroyed a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an6.50:1.6: Suppose there was a tree that lacked branches and foliage.
translation_text.an6.50:1.7: Its shoots, bark, softwood, and heartwood would not grow to fullness.
translation_text.an6.50:1.8: In the same way, when there is no sense restraint, one who lacks sense restraint has destroyed a vital condition for ethical conduct. …
translation_text.an6.50:1.9: One who lacks disillusionment and dispassion has destroyed a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an6.50:2.1: When there is sense restraint, one who has fulfilled sense restraint has fulfilled a vital condition for ethical conduct.
translation_text.an6.50:2.2: When there is ethical conduct, one who has fulfilled ethical conduct has fulfilled a vital condition for right immersion.
translation_text.an6.50:2.3: When there is right immersion, one who has fulfilled right immersion has fulfilled a vital condition for true knowledge and vision.
translation_text.an6.50:2.4: When there is true knowledge and vision, one who has fulfilled true knowledge and vision has fulfilled a vital condition for disillusionment and dispassion.
translation_text.an6.50:2.5: When there is disillusionment and dispassion, one who has fulfilled disillusionment and dispassion has fulfilled a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an6.50:2.6: Suppose there was a tree that was complete with branches and foliage. Its shoots, bark, softwood, and heartwood would all grow to fullness.
translation_text.an6.50:2.7: In the same way, when there is sense restraint, one who has fulfilled sense restraint has fulfilled a vital condition for ethical conduct. …
translation_text.an6.50:2.8: One who has fulfilled disillusionment and dispassion has fulfilled a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.”
